_description: Sample trajectory.json showing the shape /web-evolve writes to a project's .evolution/ directory across successive runs. This file demonstrates the 'continuous improvement loop' claim made in the skill frontmatter — each new invocation reads this state and advances one tier (per Cardinal Rule: Phase A.0 picks target based on current_score + history). The real trajectory.json lives in {project_path}/.evolution/trajectory.json, NOT inside this skill folder. This sample is for reviewers and new users to see the expected shape.
_emitted_by: iter-step.sh + append-retro.sh (script-only writes per Cardinal Rule 2)
_purpose: demonstrate the loop is real, not just SKILL.md prose

runs.halt_reason: void_count: 3 invisible iters caught by per-iter-gates ssim threshold (0.985)

gate_results.gate_a_route_around: FAIL — only 2 refinement skills fired; tier 98 requires ≥6

runs.retro_action: Run #4 will revert run #3 commits + retry with reduced target (95) or different signature direction

_invariants: Each run advances one tier from the previous final_score, OR re-attempts the same tier on a failed run
phase_f_status is one of: complete, halted_before_phase_c, halted_phase_a, halted_phase_b, halted_phase_c
All three gates (A/B/C) must PASS for merged_to_main: true
void_count >= 3 in any single run triggers HALT (per Cardinal Rule 9)
writer attribution recorded in .hashes.json proves orchestrator did not author trajectory.json directly
